sections.title: Methodology
sections.content: Market sizing uses triangulated estimates from three independent approaches: top-down (global market to addressable segment), bottom-up (unit economics x installation count), and trade flow analysis (Comtrade + Eurostat). All calculations shown explicitly. [SRC: web_search]

sections.title: Global TAM: Total Addressable Market
sections.content:
### Top-Down Approach

| Parameter | Value | Source |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Global elevator installations per year (new) | 700,000 units | [SRC: web_search] Elevator World 2024 |
| Average guide rail length per elevator | 80 m (car rails + counterweight rails, 10-story average) | Industry standard |
| Average weight per meter (mix of T70-T140) | 10.2 kg/m | Weighted average from ISO 7465 profiles |
| Total guide rail steel per year | 700,000 x 80m x 10.2 kg/m = 571,200 t | Calculation |
| Modernization / replacement demand | ~30% of new demand = 171,360 t | Industry estimate |
| **Total global demand (tons)** | **~742,560 t/year** | |
| Average selling price | EUR 1,400/t (global blended) | Industry estimate |
| **Global TAM (EUR)** | **EUR 1.04B** (guide rail steel only) | |
| Including fittings, brackets, kits | x 1.8 multiplier | Industry ratio |
| **Global TAM incl. accessories (EUR)** | **EUR 1.87B** | |

### Bottom-Up Validation

| Region | New Installations | Rail Demand (t) | Value (EUR M) | Source |
|--------|------------------|----------------|---------------|--------|
| China | 350,000 | 285,600 | 342 | [SRC: web_search] China Elevator Assoc. |
| Europe (EU27+UK+CH) | 95,000 | 77,520 | 116 | [SRC: web_search] ELA statistics |
| North America | 55,000 | 44,880 | 72 | [SRC: web_search] NAEC data |
| Middle East & Africa | 45,000 | 36,720 | 51 | Industry estimate |
| Asia-Pacific (ex-China) | 110,000 | 89,760 | 126 | Industry estimate |
| South America | 25,000 | 20,400 | 27 | Industry estimate |
| Rest of World | 20,000 | 16,320 | 22 | Industry estimate |
| **Total** | **700,000** | **571,200** | **756** | |
| + Modernization (~30%) | | 171,360 | 227 | |
| **Bottom-up total** | | **742,560** | **~983** | |

**Cross-check**: Top-down EUR 1.04B vs bottom-up EUR 983M = 6% delta. **ACCEPTABLE.**

sections.title: European SAM: Serviceable Addressable Market
sections.content:
| Parameter | Value | Calculation |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| European new installations | 95,000 units/year | ELA statistics |
| + Modernization (30%) | 28,500 units | |
| Total European elevator demand | 123,500 units/year | |
| Guide rail demand (t) | 123,500 x 80m x 10.2 kg/m = 100,778 t | |
| T-rail profiles only (excl. machined rails, drawn guides) | ~75% = 75,584 t | |
| **European T-rail SAM (tons)** | **~75,600 t/year** | |
| **European T-rail SAM (EUR)** | **EUR 113M** (at EUR 1,500/t European price) | |

### DACH Region Focus

| Parameter | Value | Calculation |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| DACH new installations | Germany 18,000 + Austria 3,500 + Switzerland 3,000 = 24,500 | [SRC: web_search] VDMA, TUeV |
| + Modernization (35% — older building stock) | 8,575 | |
| Total DACH demand | 33,075 units/year | |
| Guide rail demand (t) | 33,075 x 80m x 10.2 kg/m = 26,989 t | |
| T-rail profiles only (75%) | 20,242 t | |
| T89/B share (60% of T-rails) | 12,145 t | |
| **DACH T-rail SAM (tons)** | **~20,200 t/year** | |
| **DACH T89/B SAM (tons)** | **~12,100 t/year** | |
| **DACH T-rail SAM (EUR)** | **EUR 30.3M** | |
| **DACH T89/B SAM (EUR)** | **EUR 18.2M** | |

sections.title: SOM: Serviceable Obtainable Market
sections.content:
### Year 1 (Market Entry)

| Assumption | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| Target customers | 2-3 regional elevator OEMs/installers | Qualification cycle: 6-9 months |
| Share of customer's rail spend | 15-25% (secondary supplier position) | Standard risk diversification |
| Average customer rail consumption | 500-800 t/year | Mid-size DACH elevator installer |
| ZOLLERN Year 1 volume | 150-300 t | 2 customers x 15% share x 600 t avg |
| **Year 1 SOM (tons)** | **~200 t** | Conservative |
| **Year 1 SOM (EUR)** | **EUR 300K** | At EUR 1,500/t |

### Year 3 (Established Supplier)

| Assumption | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| Target customers | 5-8 customers (DACH + Benelux) | EN 81 certification opens doors |
| Share of customer's rail spend | 25-40% (primary/dual-source) | Proven quality track record |
| Average customer rail consumption | 600 t/year | Mix of mid and large accounts |
| ZOLLERN Year 3 volume | 750-1,920 t | 5-8 customers x 30% share x 600 t avg |
| **Year 3 SOM (tons)** | **~1,200 t** | Base case |
| **Year 3 SOM (EUR)** | **EUR 1.8M** | At EUR 1,500/t |

### Year 5 (Scale)

| Assumption | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| Target customers | 10-15 (DACH + Western Europe) | Direct OEM relationships (Schindler, KONE regional) |
| Share achievable | 8-12% of DACH SAM | Proven player, competitive pricing |
| **Year 5 SOM (tons)** | **~2,000-2,400 t** | |
| **Year 5 SOM (EUR)** | **EUR 3.0-3.6M** | |

sections.title: Summary Table
sections.content:
| Metric | Tons/Year | EUR/Year | Geography |
|--------|----------|---------|-----------|
| Global TAM (all guide rail steel) | 742,560 | EUR 1.04B | Global |
| European SAM (T-rail profiles) | 75,600 | EUR 113M | EU27+UK+CH |
| DACH SAM (T-rail profiles) | 20,200 | EUR 30.3M | DE+AT+CH |
| DACH SAM (T89/B only) | 12,100 | EUR 18.2M | DE+AT+CH |
| SOM Year 1 | 200 | EUR 300K | DACH |
| SOM Year 3 | 1,200 | EUR 1.8M | DACH + Benelux |
| SOM Year 5 | 2,200 | EUR 3.3M | DACH + W. Europe |
